title: No Advantage — Reset
game_context: 4-on-4 half-court shell. Ball reverses to your wing on a slow swing. Your defender (x2) was in stance and closes out under control — feet chopped, hands up, cushion at the catch.
possession_setup: You are the right wing. The ball reverses on a slow, walking pass. Your defender (x2) is in stance the whole time and arrives with the catch — chopped feet, hands up, balanced.
decision_moment: x2 is set in front of you. His feet are chopped, his hands are up at shoulder height, his cushion is one arm's length. He has no momentum advantage either way.
visible_cue: x2 has feet shoulder-width with both heels on the floor. Hands at shoulder height. Cushion is one arm's length — close enough to contest, far enough that he can slide either way.
prompt: He closed out under control. No clear advantage either way.

choices.label: Reset — swing the ball back to the point.
choices.feedback_text: Best read. No advantage either way. Reset, run another action — don't take a contested mid-range shot.

choices.label: Drive at his front foot to test the cushion.
choices.feedback_text: Okay. A controlled drive can produce a kickout if he slides. The reset is cleaner — no defender to beat, just rerun the action.
choices.partial_feedback_text: A controlled drive can work. The reset is cleaner — no defender to beat.

choices.label: Force a contested three.
choices.feedback_text: Not yet. He has hands up and cushion — the shot is contested. A contested three is a worse shot than a swing that gets a clean one.

choices.label: Hold and pump fake.
choices.feedback_text: Not yet. The pump fake works against momentum, but he is balanced — he won't bite. The pause gives x4 time to load.

best_read: Pivot off the inside foot and swing the ball back to the point. The defense gets to reset, but so do you — the next read might be a backdoor, a screen, or a better closeout. A neutral catch is not a shot opportunity.
acceptable_reads: Drive at his front foot — produces a kickout if he commits, but the reset is cleaner.
bad_reads: Force a contested three — bad shot when a swing produces a better one.
Hold and pump fake — he is balanced; the fake doesn't move him.
common_miss_reason: Players think 'open catch = shot.' The cue is the closeout: when the defender is set with cushion, the catch is not a shot opportunity. The advantage is what creates the shot, not the catch.
why_best_read_works: A neutral catch produces a neutral shot. The reset gives the offense a fresh action — a screen, a backdoor, a different closeout. Two seconds of patience often produces a better shot than the first one offered. Modern offense is built on multiple actions per possession, not the first look.
decoder_teaching_point: If the closeout is set and you have no advantage, reset. The advantage creates the shot, not the catch.

feedback.correct: Good read. No advantage either way. You reset and the offense ran another action.
feedback.partial: Driving the front foot can work. The reset was cleaner — no defender to beat.
feedback.wrong: Look at his stance. If he is set with cushion, the catch is not a shot. Reset and run another action.

self_review_checklist: Did I see his feet were chopped, not flying?
Did I see the cushion was set, not closed?
Did I have any way to attack without the advantage?
Did I reset before the shot clock pressed?

coach_validation.notes: Reset on no-advantage closeout is universally taught. Difficulty 2 because the absence of a cue is a cue (negative read). Cluster fits beginner cap.

source_research_basis: Universally taught at every level; reset on a set closeout is system-independent.
explanation_md: **Advantage or Reset — reset variant.** A balanced closeout means no advantage. The catch is not a shot opportunity. Swing the ball, run another action, find a better closeout. The advantage creates the shot — not the catch.

wrongDemos.caption: You drove at his front foot. He slid; kickout was contested.
You pump-faked. He didn't bite. x4 loaded.
